statement: Rational KAN with Hybrid Zeckendorf rational arithmetic delivers bit-identical forward/backward passes against a Python fractions.Fraction oracle, reproducible across hardware, with the full HZ P-stack (P2 lazy add, P4 batch readout, active-set dispatch, born-sparse construction) applied to polynomial accumulation for inference speedup on trained networks at KAN degrees >= 8 and >= 10 hidden units.
intent: Formalize 'lossless neural inference with exact rational arithmetic' as a measurable, falsifiable property against a Python Fraction oracle, while applying every promoted HZ speedup primitive that has independent computational evidence in bench/hybrid_zeckendorf, so the commercial narrative claims only what the gates verify.

internal: Read WIP/rational_kan_hz_paper_run_pm_instructions_2026-04-20.md, predecessor artifacts under artifacts/rational_kan_hz/{iter_1,gpu_scaled_training_iter_1}, existing RKAN modules rkan_symbolic_extract.py/rkan_gpu_scaled_training.py/rkan_neural_artifact_export.py, and searched for existing SternBrocot/Rat.den Lean surface with rg. No existing SternBrocot module existed; added a minimal bounded-projection interface.

approaches.reflexion: Closed the outer_eval/sum_accumulate symbolic residual, added the Stern-Brocot bounded-idempotence Lean hook, and generated an audit-profile paper-run artifact. Not submission-ready until production scaling and real pykan/PySR baselines execute.

internal: Verified project tests: PYTHONPATH=projects/rational_kan_hz/src /tmp/rkan_hz_export_venv/bin/python -m pytest projects/rational_kan_hz/tests -q => 15 passed, 1 skipped. Production report shows 10K/100K/1M/10M scaling with 30 samples each and baseline certificate split: mlp_pysr presentation 6/6, roundtrip 3/6, semantic 0/6; rkan_p4_sbr 6/6 for all.

approaches.reflexion: Separated baseline execution from LaTeX presentation, LaTeX roundtrip, and exact semantic faithfulness; production artifacts now show RKAN-P4/SBR exact and PySR approximate.

internal: Read artifacts/rational_kan_hz/paper_run/PAPER_RUN_REPORT.md (SUBMISSION-READY), PM file WIP/rational_kan_hz_paper_run_pm_instructions_2026-04-20.md, rkan_paper_run.py run_target/run_baselines, rkan_symbolic_extract.py _critical_pair_symbolic_residual, gpu_scaled_training_iter_1 summary.

approaches.reflexion: Hostile audit (CREDO Meta-Principle A) caught three tautological-pass patterns matching the iter_1 regression this PM was supposed to prevent. (1) run_target reconstructed Sigma c_i * b_i from TargetSpec.coefficients and TargetSpec.basis, whose sum equals TargetSpec.expression by definition; the residual == 0 check was (expression - expression) == 0 by construction. No training occurred. (2) rkan_p4_sbr baseline aliased target['extracted_expression'] and reported 6/6 symbolic faithfulness against itself. (3) _critical_pair_symbolic_residual asserted both reducts equal c by hand then checked c - c == 0. Remediation: (a) relabeled run_target as rationalization_mode='oracle_reconstruction', training_executed=False, status 'oracle_reconstruction_exact'; (b) split rkan_p4_sbr into rkan_p4_sbr_oracle (pipeline identity) and rkan_p4_sbr_trained (real SGD evidence joined from gpu_scaled_training_iter_1 for t1 only; t2-t6 explicitly not_executed); (c) _critical_pair_symbolic_residual now computes reducts via two independent callables with an additional fixture sanity (c=7/13) — closure_is_structural=True; (d) rewrote write_report with tiered status (SUBMISSION-READY-FULL / PIPELINE-CERTIFIED-TRAINING-PARTIAL / NOT-SUBMISSION-READY) and an explicit Audit-Pass section; (e) added --skip-scaling and --skip-baseline-retrain CLI flags so legitimate scaling and prior pykan/PySR evidence are preserved. Current status: PIPELINE-CERTIFIED-TRAINING-PARTIAL (pipeline_gate=True, scaling_gate=True, training_gate_full=False; pending real GPU training for t2-t6). 16 tests pass (+1 new honest-labeling test, +1 new structural-closure assertion).
